name: gmod-glua-ls
description: Language Server for Garry's Mod GLua

commands.title: Entity Explorer: Search Entity Table Values

languageModelTools.name: search_glua_docs
languageModelTools.userDescription: Search Garry's Mod GLua API documentation for functions, classes, and other symbols.
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Search the Garry's Mod GLua API documentation and local codebase index for functions, methods, classes, and constants. 

Query syntax:
- Type:method (e.g. "Entity:GetPos", "Player:GetName") searches for a specific method on a class
- type.field (e.g. "hook.Add", "math.max") searches for global table members or static methods
- Plain word (e.g. "Player", "GetPos", "hook") searches class names, globals, and all class methods with that name

Returns full documentation including signatures, parameter names and types, return types, and description text - the same information shown when hovering over a symbol in the editor.

Use this tool when: writing or reviewing GLua code and you need to know what arguments a function takes, what it returns, or whether it exists. Always prefer using this tool over guessing API signatures.

Do not use for: general Lua language questions (only for Garry's Mod specific API and the current workspace's code).

query.description: The API symbol or concept to search for. Examples: 'Entity:GetPos', 'hook.Add', 'ents', 'Player:GetName', 'physics'

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return. Default is 10, maximum is 20.

languageModelTools.name: gmod_run_lua
languageModelTools.userDescription: Execute a Lua chunk in the active Garry's Mod debug session.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Runs arbitrary Lua code in the currently active Garry's Mod debug session using the selected runtime realm (server, client, or shared). Use this when the user asks to test Lua behavior live in-game, query runtime state, or perform controlled debugger-side execution. This tool requires an active GMod debug session and returns a formatted summary including realm, success status, and diagnostics.

lua.description: Lua source code to execute in Garry's Mod.

realm.description: Optional runtime realm. If omitted, uses the extension's current selected GMod realm.

languageModelTools.name: gmod_run_command
languageModelTools.userDescription: Run a Garry's Mod console command in the active debug session.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Executes a Garry's Mod console command through the active debugger transport. Use this when the user explicitly asks to run a console command (for example, convars, debug helpers, or command-driven runtime checks). Requires an active GMod debug session and returns a formatted result with realm context, status, and diagnostics.

command.description: Console command text to execute in Garry's Mod.

languageModelTools.name: gmod_run_file
languageModelTools.userDescription: Dispatch a Lua file path to be include()'d in Garry's Mod.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Dispatches a Lua file path for execution in Garry's Mod via debugger control (equivalent to MCP run_file behavior). Use this when the user wants to run an existing script file in server/client/shared realm without copying file contents into a command. Requires an active GMod debug session and returns formatted status and diagnostics.

path.description: File path to dispatch for execution in Garry's Mod.

realm.description: Optional runtime realm. If omitted, uses the extension's current selected GMod realm.

languageModelTools.name: gmod_get_output
languageModelTools.userDescription: Return recent buffered Garry's Mod output entries.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns recent buffered Garry's Mod output log entries captured by the extension's debugger event stream, including runtime output and control-command summaries. Use this when the user asks for runtime output or recent command activity. Does not require an active debug session. The response includes formatted bullet entries with timestamp, source, level, and message.

limit.description: Maximum number of recent output entries to return. Default 50.

languageModelTools.name: gmod_get_errors
languageModelTools.userDescription: Return recent buffered Garry's Mod error entries.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns recent buffered Garry's Mod error log entries captured by backend/control error handling and command-rejection tracking. Use this when the user asks for failures, backend errors, or rejected control commands. Does not require an active debug session. The response includes formatted bullet entries with timestamp, source, level, and message.

limit.description: Maximum number of recent error entries to return. Default 50.

languageModelTools.name: gmod_get_debug_state
languageModelTools.userDescription: Return the current Garry's Mod debugger state snapshot.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the full debugger state snapshot, including active session presence, session metadata, selected realm, language server state, MCP host health, and buffered output/error counts. Use this tool to diagnose debugger connectivity and runtime state before attempting command execution. Does not require an active debug session and returns the state as JSON.

realm.description: Runtime realm for debugger control commands
Runtime realm for debugger control commands

editor/context.when: resourceLangId == lua && editorHasSelection && inDebugMode && debugType == gluals_gmod

gluals.gmod.autoLoadAnnotations.markdownDescription: Automatically download and load Garry's Mod GLuaLS annotations as a core library. Annotations are downloaded from the `gluals-annotations` branch of the gmod-luals-addon repository. Disable this if you want to manage annotations manually or use a custom set.

gluals.gmod.settingsAutoSave.markdownDescription: Automatically save changes made in the GLua Settings editor to the workspace .gluarc.json after a short delay. Disabled by default to reduce accidental overwrites; use the editor's manual save button when off.

gluals.gmod.debugRealm.markdownDescription: Lua execution realm used by debugger control commands (`runLua`, `runFile`, `breakHere`, `waitIDE`).

gluals.gmod.vgui.codeLensEnabled.description: Enable Code Lenses for VGUI panel definitions

gluals.gmod.vgui.inlayHintEnabled.description: Enable Inlay Hints for VGUI panel definitions

gluals.gmod.mcp.enabled.markdownDescription: Enable the local authenticated MCP host for GMod debug tool execution.

gluals.gmod.mcp.port.markdownDescription: Port for the local MCP host (`0` chooses a random ephemeral port).

gluals.gmod.mcp.authToken.markdownDescription: Optional MCP auth token override. Leave empty to generate a secure local token automatically.

gluals.gmod.mcp.rateLimitPerMinute.markdownDescription: Maximum authenticated MCP requests accepted per minute per client address.

gluals.ls.debugPort.markdownDescription: Look for language server on this port instead of starting it from an executable

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with Garry's Mod LS
walkthroughs.description: Setup the Garry's Mod language server and debugger.

steps.title: Diagnostic Wizard
steps.description:
Run the diagnostic wizard to verify workspace paths and dependencies.

[Run Wizard](command:gluals.gmod.onboarding.start)

steps.title: Install Type Annotations
steps.description:
Download GMod API annotations to enable autocomplete and type hints.

[Download Annotations](command:gluals.gmod.updateAnnotations)

steps.title: Setup Live Debugging
steps.description:
Install the debugger module to the local Garry's Mod instance.

[Configure Debugger](command:gluals.gmod.configureDebugger)

steps.title: Open Settings Panel
steps.description:
Open the Settings Panel to configure `.gluarc.json` formatting and diagnostic behaviors.

[Open GLua Settings](command:gluals.gmod.openSettings)

scripts.package:dev:win32-x64: npm run ls:build:release && npm run prepare:dev:win32-x64 && npx vsce package -o vscode-gmod-ls-dev-win32-x64.vsix --target win32-x64
scripts.package:release:win32-x64: npm run prepare:release:win32-x64 && npx vsce package -o vscode-gmod-ls-win32-x64.vsix --target win32-x64
